rt2-3-2.title: Hands-on: cure a blurry radiograph
rt2-3-2.intro: Poor definition is a geometry problem: Ug = f x OFD / SOD. This setup starts badly blurred by a 4 mm source sitting close over a big film gap — cure it by closing the gap and backing the source off until the ghost radiograph sharpens past the 0.4 mm spec.

tasks.label: Shrink Ug to 0.4 mm or less and sharpen the image
tasks.hint: Ug = f x OFD / SOD: first drag the film gap toward zero, then pull the source farther back.

tasks.label: Set the wire IQI on the source-side weld surface
tasks.hint: Drag the IQI onto the top, source-facing surface — film-side placement is rejected.

rt2-3-3.title: Hands-on: land film density in the code window
rt2-3-3.intro: Density is measured, not eyeballed: read the console's density number like a densitometer and land it inside the 2.0-3.0 window (2.0 is the gamma code minimum). Then prove why measurement matters — 30 days of Ir-192 decay drifts the density low, and you must catch it and re-expose.

tasks.label: Expose so the density readout lands between 2.0 and 3.0
tasks.hint: Density rises with exposure E = A x t — lengthen the time until D clears the 2.0 gamma minimum.

tasks.label: After 30 days, re-expose to put density back in window
tasks.hint: Activity halves every 73.8 days, so the same density needs a longer exposure time than before.
